image.alt: Ancient carved bust of a woman wearing a royal headdress

attribution.fileTitle: File:Bust of Cleopatra VII - Altes Museum - Berlin - Germany 2017.jpg
attribution.author: José Luiz

rounds.revealFact: Cleopatra VII was the last active ruler of the Ptolemaic Kingdom of Egypt, reigning until 30 BCE.

image.alt: Painting of soldiers in Civil War-era uniforms in combat on open farmland

attribution.fileTitle: File:Thure de Thulstrup - L. Prang and Co. - Battle of Gettysburg - Restoration by Adam Cuerden (cropped).jpg
attribution.author: Thure de Thulstrup / Adam Cuerden

clues: Two armies collided almost by accident, then fought for three days over the surrounding hills and ridges.
A massive infantry charge across open ground toward a stone wall ended in heavy losses for the attackers.

events.label: The Ottoman Turks capture Constantinople, ending the Byzantine Empire
Martin Luther posts his 95 Theses, sparking the Reformation
The German states unify into a single empire
The United Nations is founded in San Francisco

image.alt: A large airship engulfed in flames as it descends toward a landing field

rounds.prompt: The RMS Titanic sinks in the North Atlantic after striking an iceberg.
rounds.revealFact: More than 1,500 people died when the supposedly unsinkable liner went down on her maiden voyage.

rounds.prompt: Where did the Khmer Empire build its capital?
rounds.revealFact: At its height Angkor was one of the largest pre-industrial urban complexes on Earth, anchored by the temple of Angkor Wat.
